reviews.review_msg: He's a great Prof, doesn't really know anything about Economics though. Sometimes I feel like he isn't even a teacher and is really a student pretending to be a professor.
The lectures are boring but he is a fair marker
When it comes to lectures alone, Allen is one of the best ever. However, his absence of using Canvas was a bummer, and it made studying difficult due to relying heavily on his textbook.

Got A+ after reading the textbook, doing all odd problems, going to lectures and tutorials. Lectures were interesting, and textbook was relatively easy to read. Although I found some exam/textbook problems to be ambigious/confusing. Useful and effective class to develop economical thinking.

Doug Allen is honestly a great professor. His lectures are super interesting and engaging and hes an amazing speaker. His textbook that he assigns is tedious yet not difficult. Students that complain about this professor are unmotivated students that do not show up to lectures or tutorials and skip on weekly readings.

Hardest class ive ever taken even for a guy of my caliber good teacher but if you want a easy good grade prob dont take this guy my friends at UBC and SFU who taken microeconomics said they've never heard of the things this guy talks about, my friends got easy A's with other profs and couldn't do a single question in the textbook the teacher gave..
Simple class for a guy of my caliber. Played brawl stars every lecture and ended up with a B-. If you're even remotely intelligent in some capacity you're essentially chillin.
Doesn't have any form of canvas or online classroom, which just makes the class harder. There are no practice exams, no extra learning material, and he also makes you buy a $100 textbook.

The material is pretty interesting and I thought I understood the it until the first midterm which I did not pass. He lets the weight go to the final instead which is why I did well also he curves it a lot. He is really easy to contact but the exams are kind of hard and you really have to understand the concepts not just memorize.

i feel like he writes his own rmps (ik he reads them). despite going to all lectures and locking in, i didn't do good. he's looking for one answer and one answer only even if the entire class agrees on a different answer. tutorials felt like a waste of time. skip at all costs.

This class honestly changed my life. Doug's a great dude and all his haters are the people who put no effort. He tells you at the start of the course that if you attend his lectures you'll pass the class, which is what I did and got a better grade than I expected. Grading is sorta tough but he curves the final marks decently. He's funny asf too

Very good professor. Content is difficult but go to lectures and keep up with your readings and practice and you should be fine. I will say, it is definitely a tough course but the interesting lectures are worth it in my opinion.

To future students: PLEASE LOCK IN AS SOON AS POSSIBLE! DO ALL QUESTIONS IN THE TEXTBOOK! (It's the hardest 100 course in SFU)

Allen is a great lecturer, but personally, a lot of his beliefs are disagreeable/overly simplistic and you need to use them to answer conceptual questions. 

Oh yeah, there is a $100 textbook you need to buy.
Its a rewarding course if you put in the effort. I found his lectures the most engaging and fun to attend. would recommend this course.

He's a very passionate prof that genuinely cares. I found his lectures very interesting and very fun to attend. Make sure to consistently practice and review as it's a course that you can easily fall behind. If you have any questions go to his office hours as he explains questions very well
Very passionate about the things he talk about. Very good at what he does but can overcomplicate it sometimes. Midterm and final exam was harder than expectation.

He's a terrible teacher, ignore every other person on this that says he's good. His teaching methods are old and outdated and his TA's are useless. Class makes no sense in the way he teaches it, makes you buy a 100$ textbook just for it to not help whatsoever. Midterm and final are horrendous and he is useless.
He made us buy his 100$ textbook, and assigns the homeworks based on that, the book is useless and has a lots of issues.
dont take this class with Doug
he makes this class much harder than it actually is. Ignore the other reviews he is not good at all.
Tough, vague lecture, yapping a lot

Some of the most amazing and engaging lectures I have ever been to. The way he connects economics principles to modern events is great. Some setbacks I would mention,
a) he has his own textbook that he re-releases every couple sems forcing you to buy it (albeit, it's a decent textbook)
b) his textbook doesn't have an index
c) doesn't share slides

Doug is very passionate about economics and explains the concepts very well so lectures are never boring. However, when it comes to his exams, the questions are quite hard (similar to his textbook questions) but you will be saved by the insane curve. Gives you the opportunity to leave exam questions blank to get 1/3 of the mark

Honestly, if you as a business student find this class extremely challenging, you might not be cut out for theprogram. Doug Allen prepares you for university-level difficulty. People are mad that he tests you on what he says in lecture? That's the whole point of university. Go to the lectures, take notes, do practice problems, and you will be okay.

Douglas Allen is the best professor I have ever met in my life, with his insightful and engaging lectures that helped me develop the ability to think rationally. Follow this plan: read each chapter, do review questions, and solve tutorial problem sets. As a non‑native speaker, this approach earned me an A+. So impressive lecture, I'll never forget.
Doug Allen is the GOAT econ 103 prof

He's a good professor but intimidating. His midterm and final exams are difficult, although he does curve his marks. I've seen classmates abuse leaving questions blank on the exam as it grants you 30% and still passing. It's a hard course but doable if you put in the effort.
Its a really good course, questions teach you to think critically and analyze scenarios which can be helpful for your other classes. Lectures are pretty solid too, and he's quiet funny

If you focus solely on what he teaches he's a good teacher, as soon as you delve into his worldview, you start running into problems. Just like in economics, he justifies his worldview but his worldview is wildly out of the norms and his justifications involve faulty research. So just focus on the topics, don't ever question his perspective.
He's a really good prof, but oh boy, are the tests challenging. Take it if you genuinely want to learn econ concepts, but don't expect an easy ride. Worth it overall. His lectures are great

His textbooks need to be written better. Some questions will have "econ" related scenarios while some are talking about the government and the answer has absolute nothing to do with the concept. His answer keys sound like itll make sense in his head but the explanation needs to be more clear

The best prof I've ever had. His tests are quite hard, but he will genuinely change the way you think, and you will be better off for it. I have ADHD and this is the only time I've ever been able to listen to lectures non-stop. Doug's course is phenomenal - don't listen to those who tell you it's too hard - it's hard, but worth it.

I took Econ 103 with Doug in Fall 2023 last year. He's undeniably a very good lecturer and definitely goes out of his way to make the course engaging. TA's are also helpful in tutorials. Exams are difficult but curved generously. Work hard and grind the textbook, and you'll do well.

Best introduction to economics I could possibly imagine, Doug was a passionate and engaging professor. I am able to apply the principles we learned to everything in my life after taking the course with him. Unfortunately, many people taking this course are in their first semester and are not at the level required to deeply understand the topics.

Doug is incredibly entertaining to listen to. I very much so enjoyed his lectures and thought that the information he provided makes sense and is very useful. I will be continuing in the field of economics, and am very glad I got introduced to it through him. 
Be ready for a challenge, the material is quite heavy and requires critical analysis.

Doug's lectures are engaging, but the class is quite challenging. The textbook he wrote can be tricky, so rewatching recorded lectures and using tools like unstuckstudy ai alongside the material are really helpful. Attend all classes, do the practice problems, and you'll learn a lot despite the difficulty.

This was a tough class. Tests and weekly homework questions were very difficult, but lectures were engaging and were where I learned the most. The textbook is self-written and hard to get through. Taking high school economics and/or having strong math skills will be beneficial to you. TAs can help your grade so make sure to attend tutorials.

As a 1st year (beedie) student I heard a lot of mixed reviews about Doug. But I enjoyed his class, the only downside was having to buy his textbook. The material itself can get confusing but it's always easy to ask Doug or the TAs (Richmond is goated) for help. There is a good amount of reading but I relied more on rewatching the recorded lectures.

Doug Allen was great as a lecturer - funny and engaging. One major con is having to buy his own textbook for nearly $100 CAD. The textbook itself can be a bit confusing at times. 
Exams were very tough. Overall, it was an okay experience, but I would not recommend taking this class as an elective.

Douglas Allen is evidently very knowledgable and passionate about economics. He always made time to answer questions and was easy to reach. To do well you need to attend every lecture and read the textbook. The course is content heavy and I did have to do outside research to complete the problem sets. Be prepared to work hard...but it's rewarding.

Doug is a passionate prof, and it shows. To get the most from lectures, do the readings in-advance. In general, I found his textbook to be very helpful for learning and practice. I highly recommend doing all the practice problems from the book to prepare for exams since these are very similar to problems asked on exams. Hard work required.
I am now an economics major after taking his micro class in 2006.  I loved his style of teaching.

No need to attend lectures. I only went like 5 times throughout the semester and I got A-. TA is pretty useless (language barrier). Materials are straight from textbook. Assignments are done online (can be tricky).
the best!
He's alright, I guess.

She is a very kind person super engaging and enthusiastic however her quizzes are so hard!!! Eveb if I studied I didn't knew where the questions were coming from. She also has extra assignments to do on your own that are "complimentary" but it doesn't seem like it.

Probably the best professor for this subject. Practise a lot and do the extra example she posts and you would be fine. The grades are curved and the average is C+. If she can teach economics to someone as dumb as me she can teach economics to anyone.

She is so fun when it comes to explaining things as She will provide you with reallife example. She is a good professor. Quiz might be hard sometimes. She gave lots of practice questions for those who want to learn more. Make sure to do all the extra question to get a good score in quiz! Overall, She is great, quizzess can be hard.

Shauna is hilarious and explains the concepts very well. Her notes are clear and she also provides you with extra material to practice. Make sure you do her practice questions and pay attention in class and you should be fine. Exams are fair but a bit tricky.

If you have trouble learning an entire course from a textbook, don't take this guy's class. The guy basically left us a blank canvas page except for the eight assignments which had nothing in common with the two midterms and final exam. No posted notes, not slides. Projected, hand-written notes and straight yapping for 2 hours twice a week.

Well meaning guy but not a great professor. Concepts in the exams aren't in the textbook or homework, harsh grader with little feedback, and also not super clear about what's in the exams. You NEED the textbook for this course and u have to be ready to teach urself because his lectures aren't super great either. Also he doesn't curve, only scale
LONG LIVE THE KING. this guys an absolute legend
It was my first ECON course, so studying was challenging. Her exams were tough!!! However, she's a super good professor and greatly supported my class.

Jiamin Liu is one of the best teachers I have ever had in my education. She is really caring and willing to help students with their problems and her lectures are really AMAZING. Lastly, all you need to learn well in this course is DOING HOMEWORK.
A very lovely instructor. She is totally caring, open-minded and very attentive to students. I usually have troubles during the first phase of the course, but she helped me to get through it easily.

Pros:
-detail notes (no need textbook)
-clearly explain 
-1 midterm only 
-midterm and final are quite easy if you go over her notes
Cons:
-quizzes are so much harder than midterm and final. it will double minus your score. however, you can read through the quizzes before the exam as she might use some of the concepts.
easy course

Kristin's style of teaching Economics is very good. I am not a good Eco student but taking it with her made me a bright Eco student. The biggest drawback of taking her is her quizzes which are of 75% of whole grade and inside those quizzes True False are negative marking. So if you cope up in the quizzes from the very beginning, Kristin is for you.

A ridiculous grading system, attendance was optional. Have a quiz every single week and while aware that the average score was not well, did not have the effort of going through the quiz one more time and explain the work process. At the very least, she will answer your questions if you ask and does that quite clearly

Basically, taking her class is just listening to her lecture and taking notes, fortunately, she will give all the notes and all you need to do is fill in the blank. The course is curved so remember to knock your classmates out before the final exam^^. If you are at fic pick Kristin quick. She is the best you can get.
Do well in every quiz and assignment review your notes regularly it's really easy to achieve high marks in her class especially online courses.

Super easy to follow teacher, her lectures consist of easy fill in the blank notes with tons of examples. Made the course easy to get an A and was very nice and helpful with any questions. Exams were primarily multiple choice with a few graphs. As long as you understand basic linear graphs, you'll be good. Definitely GPA booster. Would take again.
good

Lectures didn't cover important topics, Prepare to self study! TOUGH GRADER, assignments given were hard. If your new to econ, good luck! Discussions very important, high percentage. Just don't mess up. Practice given was kinda helpful . Professor Greg was intimidating, felt unwelcome in class.

He becomes frustrated at students when they ask questions. Avoid at all costs. In fact I am about the graduate and I can easily say the Greg is the worst prof I have seen throughout my undergraduate career.

Took Econ 103 & 105 with this Prof. The course is generally pretty easy. Contrary to the comments below. I find this this prof to be a good person. He listens to students and trys his best to help the students understand.
Quite a good teacher. Mid-terms and exams are based mostly on lecture notes.

His class is not all that hard, however, this guy has a really bad attitude... very arrogant, not very helpful... if you go to get help, he will think you are stupid and wont help much... his notes are also very hard to read... very very very mean prof... T^T take class at your own risk !!

Wow, this class was almost too easy. His assignments are east marks and the midterm isn't that hard either. This guy's only downfall is that he may do TOO MUCH review. He spends half an hour each lecture going over the stuff he taught last lecture. If you don't do well in this course it's your fault and you shouldn't even be in University!!
Best teacher I have had by far. No other teacher has this much interest in there students success. I will definitely be taking other classes with her!!
first time i want to rate a prof on the web cuz shes the best prof i've ever met. helpful and easygoing. if u pay attemtion to the class, u will do well. and she loves feedbacks.
best prof i ever had
Best Prof i ever had, always available and very helpful

If you're weak at econ, taking it with Gordon Myers is a total nightmare. Lectures are boring and incomprehensible, the assigned textbook isn't all that helpful, and your mark is entirely made up of two midterms and a final exam. Make sure to buy the optional study guide, as that helps with studying for exams. He killed my interest in econ :(
great guy great lectures

Gordon himself was not a bad prof at all. He taught his lectures just like any other prof but it could make you fall asleep sometimes because he rambles about unrelated topics sometimes. The main issue I have is his marking scheme. He will not give partial marks at all. It's always a huge surprise when exam marks are released.

3 exams make up your mark. Exams and grading schemes are curved. Terrible lectures will put you to sleep. Not understanding and hard to get a marking error fixed. Don't bother with textbook and don't bother with lectures. Review his lecture slides and watch youtube videos. Tutorial questions are similar to exam questions. Need to go to tuts for ans

ECON103 is pretty straight forward with Myers. I would recommend skipping lectures and reading the textbook. GO TO YOUR TUTORIALS. 

To do well on exams, make sure you understand your tutorial questions, then do the end of chapter textbook questions. Just doing that for each chapter got me an A-.

Unless you buy the study guide, you probably won't do that well (unless you have A LOT of time to study). The class was based on three exams. Even if you think you understand the questions on the exams and you've got the right answers, you don't. His lectures aren't useful because he uses a lot of personal examples that don't help you understand.

Econ 103 is definitely a difficult course - with Myers - keep up with the lecture slides and tutorial questions each week. Exams were very similar to tutorial questions. Even though he's monotone, he's a very caring prof and has a lot of knowledge about economics. It can be difficult to follow him  but puts a lot of effort into the course.

Grab a blanket! myers rambles a lot about his own life+achievents in a slow monotone voice, if you did not get much sleep at night, you'll be sure to doze off in class. Classwise, he doesnt cover all the material so you must read the textbook on your own. He likes to reword the exam questions so make sure you understand content conceptually.

I did bad in this class because I have a difficult time with economics. I think prof Myers is ok, his lectures are not helpful at all. Try and utilize outside resources if you struggle with economics.

Took the class in spring 2021. Entirely test based with the grades coming from 3 midterms and the final. Great leactures and easy tests since the practice problems are identical to the test. Only major issue were some tech difficulties during the 3rd final midterm but the prof allowed students to choose if they wanted to void that midterm.

She is a very nice and encouraging teacher.
As for the exam, she focuses on assessing your ability to explain real world problems in terms of the concepts she taught in her class, not just memorizing formulas and definitions. You really need to understand the key concepts thoroughly and to be able to apply them practically to do well in her class.

I took this one year ago. You don't need to read the e-book, but you MUST purchase it because you have to subscribe to the e-book to have access to the quizzes that are worth your grade. The final exam was a disaster where the difficulty and relevancy to the course were questionable. Even doubt whether those questions are generated by ChatGPT.

You get graded on 2 midterms, 1 final, and a set of online quizzes. All are worth 25% each. The content taught in class is basic however exam questions are not at the same level. Quizzes are strenuous and the course is not well organized. You HAVE to take notes in class and there are no office hours available. I Would not suggest this professor.

Need to do your two assignments correctly (only P/F), and each 10% I lose 10% for assignment 1, that the reason why even I got good score in every exam, I couldn't get a better grade. Content is always easy, compare to the other Prof in FIC, but you still need to work hard as well. At least, no many students in her class join the cram school.

Vera is very kind and easy to approach. However, her class is different from her exams a lot. If you work hard, I think you will get a good grade. And her class is all hand-writing, it is better to take all classes. Her homework is online and if you do the practice before finishing all the assignments, you will get good marks.

Vera Lantinova is definitely one of the kindest professors Ive met so far. She is quite calm and has a soothing voice (which is sometimes a little bit too low), so listening to her is very pleasant. She definitely knows the subjects she is talking about and she is passionate about them. Definitely taking macroeconomics with her next!
she teach so simple in class and exams oh my God dammnn tough!!!
marking is fair, and in my class, 30% of class got A.
wonderful Pro. i took both ECON103 and 105 with her. she marked exams and assignments fairly. class is marked by scale. most people in my class got B

No one in the class understood her...when she asks a question no one seems to answer it "correctly" eventhough her answer is exactly the same as what we said. I totally understood the material and didn't do that well

Very nice and experienced prof, with clear notes, all the in class examples make sure to understand, as they might show up on exams, and for any relative theory related questions the professor and TA's are accessible, so practice questions alongside weekly quizzes, tutorial attendance, the exams should be relatively be familiar.

content is so easy you think you can just get by but her tests have nothing to do with the practice questions she gives. tutorials are only 5% but worth showing up as you learn more in there than the lecture. Also weekly quizzes that barely help because multiple choice is only worth a tiny amount on the tests
Lecture was pretty boring to be honest, you probably don't have to go there. She's not a harsh maker, just memorize the information from PowerPoints and you'll be okay. Very nice person tho.

Very sweet and kind lady, and was pretty chill about ChatGPT. Slides are always uploaded with annotations. Exams can seem harder since her practice quizzes are really long and repetitive, and only help with multiple choice, but they are redoable until you get full marks, which is basically extra credit.

I was a TA for this course. My opinion: focus on the problems/questions she covers in class lectures. Exam questions will reflect those.

The short questions are worth a lot of marks and are very mathematical; they are somewhat similar to past exams that will be posted on Canvas, but mostly similar to the problems covered in class.

Professor Rekkas is an amazing professor, this course compared to ECON 105 was so much easier and it was mainly because she taught so well. As long as you go to lectures and do the homework, tutorial, and sample exams you will do well in the course.

Her exams were hard but it balanced out because they were marked generously and curved. Very kind and approachable prof and she explained things well. Tutorial attendance matters and the online quizzes take very long to do as they range from 30-70 questions every week.
Marie is a very good professor, knows her topic but she sometimes get confused. Always available in office hours and always happy to help.

Pretty sure they changed how they structured the new ECON 103 tests compared to previous years as the practice exams provided were nothing like it was previously. However if you do all the quizzes she assigns and make sure you understand it, as well as understand how to calculate tax revenue and draw all the graphs, you should be able to get A.
Lectures were a bit dry at times

Gives unnecessary marked quizzes that are very long, huge timewasters that after the first twenty minutes contribute practically nothing to one's learning. Does not teach the class in an effective method and places importance on things she deems important but they really just waste your time. She is respectful.

Grade is split between weekly quizzes (unlimited attempts), midterm, and final. Everything is based off the lectures, she is passionate and entertaining. She always encourages questions and student feedback. Truly one of the best profs I've had. Midterm was easy - MC questions taken directly from quizzes. Final was the same just a little longer.

Marie is a fair grader. Midterms and finals are straightforward if you utilize resources like MyLab and Unstuck AI for study prep. Attendance is mandatory, but the content isn't overly difficult if you put in the work.

Lectures are clear, no recording or filled slides. Tutorials go through textbook example questions, not mandatory but helpful. Midterm was very easy and Final was just double the length. Marie encouraged questions and was always very good at explaining material.

Marie is a great teacher, one of the best for an introductory Econ course. There are no tricks to her class, midterms/final are just what you would expect from the tutorials/lecture material/MyLab questions.
Only downside is that the class grading is all exams. I believe 40% midterm, 60% final (took this class in Fall 2021). Strongly recommend.

She also marked very fairly. Her midterm and final were incredibly fair, stuff is taken straight from homework or in class examples. The content isn't very difficult. I was told that this class was hard but it is really easy if you remotely put effort and practice.

I was told this class is hard, but it isn't. Marie is a great teacher, she explains in a way that is very clear and understandable. The midterm was very fair, I thought it would be much harder. I didn't read the textbook, and she sometimes finishes class early. Some people may find her boring, but to get a good mark go to lectures to understand!
I would recommend going through MyLab problem sets from each chapter consistency. The lecture slides are a good summary of the formulas and graphs.

He got confused and scrambled quite a bit but as long as you pre-read and attend every lecture you will not have trouble with the course. Obviously if you skip class regularly, you are not going to do well. his exams were extremely similar to his practice exams and assignments so you know what you are getting into.

He just wrote on printed out PowerPoint slides (posted online). It was difficult to understand what he said and wrote, he also crossed out stuff, wrote over things, and did stuff wrong constantly. Studied off his assignments and managed a decent mark. I didn't open the textbook or go to lecture often. Genuine guy, just can't teach to save his life.

He's a nice guy, he brought us candy for halloween, but terrible professor. The textbook is useless, it's all in his lecture but lectures are extremely boring. I skipped quite a bit. He mixes things up, and he scratches out what he writes often which makes things confusing, also very unclear. Overall, it was horrible, thankfully I passed though.

His lectures are basically the bare minimum of what you need to learn to do well in this course. Go home, watch youtube videos, read the book, work backwards using the assignment answer keys to understand the problems. I HAVE heard that other Econ profs give much trickier exams, so if you can self teach, you might find this stuff straightforward.

Econ 103 is already a pretty boring class and frank certainly doesn't help. He spends a lot of time explaining very basic concepts, then uses questions on assignments and tests that are completely beyond what we learned in class. However, his assignments are very similar to the exams so if you can master them you will be fine.

DO NOT TAKE THIS COURSE WITH THIS PROF. He is easily one of the worst profs I have had at SFU. Lectures are dull. His notes are hard to follow as he constantly makes mistakes and crosses things out, making things extremely messy. Has trouble explaining concepts effectively and gives vague answers to questions. Look elsewhere for an econ prof.

Terrible Lecturer. Lectures are long, boring and he constantly makes mistakes while explaining concepts. Has trouble answering questions clearly. His midterm was pretty difficult and to make matters worse, his answer key was incorrect. I recommend looking for a different Econ prof. If you wan't someone who can't explain concepts, this is your guy.

-Notes are messy and hard to follow -Gives vague and unclear answers to questions -Examples in notes do not cover questions asked in assignments -Assignments are for completion marks which is fine, but they also wont say anything if your answers are completely wrong so keep that in mind if u are using them to study off of
Very confusing with his accent, if you want to fail in econ 103 like most of the class did then go ahead.

Completely boring lectures and extremely unclear. His midterm was really difficult parts of his answer key were wrong as well. If you're planning to take Econ 103, i would recommend looking somewhere else.
No Comments
She is very patient with studends.  One of the best instructor I have ever had!
She is great!  I like her because she is so willing to help!
For a couple months after that course I still kept wanting to say 'suplieded' instead of 'supplied'
she is very nice and helpful.  her notes are a bit long but they are good.

Lectures can be long and wordy. Attending is a must for weekly quizzes. The material is challenging, but Unstuck AI and the textbook help make sense of the content. Overall, a tough but manageable course.

After receiving my midterm back, I was trying to figure out why this one question was wrong (worth 9 marks) & after finally emailing her, she said that the she didn't realize the TA had regraded it & it was actually right. Had I not reached out to her, my grade would be a letter grade lower because she is careless.

She quite nice but her lectures make me feel like i was literally unlearning the stuff. Rambles on an don about easy topics for way too long and went over harder topics way to fast. Textbook was pretty good though. Midterms/final were okay but a handful of question doesn't reflect what we learnt

Ok professor but very easy class. Got by with just reading textbook a few times and doing tutorial questions. Didn't interact her much she seems intimidating at first but seems quite nice, gives a bit of my way or the highway feeling during lectures tho. Lecture was boring and there was no point going.

try taking this course with another prof. its a 3 hour lecture and by the end i feel like i am forgetting the concepts i already know. Very strict and particular about her way so even if you technically say something right she would say no not exacty correct but her way is the only correct way. I donot understand anything in her lectures
Lectures should be recorded for how much she yaps.

I wish I had read the reviews before I took her class. I am an honour roll student with straight A's and her classes are so confusing and difficult to understand. She spends so much time on one thing and then rushes through others and does things that the text book doesn't refer to yet expects you to know on exams.

She seems like a nice person, but she tends to ramble during class. She takes up a lot of the class time overexplaining certain points which led to us being behind. She does pop quizzes and weekly check-ins for attendance. Many also ended up zoning out of the class since her lectures didn't help much. Attendance isn't taken in tutorials.

Barkin needs to learn how to teach properly. Lectures were incredibly boring and the only reason you needed to attend class was to complete the quizzes. The final counted as 55% of your grade and you can tell how that affected students... Overall I suggest anyone considering taking her class to find better profs.
nice teacher

I really liked him, even though he seems a little harsh or in your face, he's just being funny, he taught the concepts so well i aced both midterms, never once read the book, he goes over every question before starting so gives u lots of time to think of answers. Definitely recommended. dont be intimidated by his accent, half of SFU profs have em!
Terrible prof
Awful professor. Does not give any help as a professor and rather cares about his own needs than the students. Condescending when teaching it
Worst teacher ever
Really good teacher, lectures arevery helpful,always teach students in a way that they can use concepts in real life easily

Very good prof.Interesting, and surprisingly, can be funny. Had little knowledge and interest in the subject before i took the course, now I love it. Midterms and final were fair, make sure you read the book. Would def recommend taking this course and having this prof.

Monotone. Great lecture notes (text is useless). TA sucks at english and teaching.  MT was alright for me but class average was like 50 and Final I'm not sure what the average was but seemed easier than MT.
Professor Liu is a gem - his lectures are clear and engaging. Stay on top of the material, use unstuck AI for extra prep, and you'll ace this class.

Junjie is one of the best econ profs at SFU/FIC. He teaches everything perfectly. He even tries to give quizzes which help to better understand the material taught. His accent might be a little problem for few but other than that there is no flaw. His tests are also quite easy and you just need to take a look on his notes to score well.
What a patient and great instructor at FIC, the school should hire more people like him! Excellent!
I skip so much class but also passed,I love you Junjie Liu. His class is fast and clear, easy for understand

He is pretty good prof with great (best) lectures. His teaching method is superb and his slides are more than enough to score well and understand concepts easily.
Quick tip: Never try to mug up concepts and try to complete all the questions!
Very nice instructor!
Best professor ever at FIC!!

It's really difficult to enroll in his classes. I guess that tells something. So I just waited patiently for a space to show up. I'm glad that I made the right decision. I did very well in midterms. I'm confident that I can do well in the final and get my first A at FIC.

put material on tests that weren't always covered in lecture ,graded by only three tests and all questions are written worth 5-10 points each so time runs out fast. choose econ 105 over this course as the curve was high and wont always help you and he records lectures in 105
Not easy to get a hold off. Knows his economics and policy
this guy is awesome if you have the chance to take him do it. you will not regret
He's just the goat
he is the goat if you can take him you should. dont consider any one else he is my goat i would take a bullet for him

THE ABSOLUTE G.O.A.T.! 

Funny, knowledgeable, and good at explaining things. I find some professors struggle to bring topics down to student level, but Yan is amazing at answering questions and ensuring thorough understanding. He does ~5 pop quizzes throughout the semester, which also mark attendance, so you should show up to class.
GOAT!! Explains stuff super clearly and is always nice n willing to help. Final was hard fr, but he curved pretty fair so it worked out. Would take again.
10/10 would take again
Had him for 103 and 105. The courses are kinda hard ngl, but he's super chill and smart. Always understands your questions and is ready to help anytime. The final was a bit too hard tho.

As a prof, hes really caring to his students and makes lectures very enjoyable. The course overall is hard, but he curves really well, so class average is around a B. While he does have a bit of an accent, his class is very well structured and he helps a lot with students questions and concerns. I would recommend taking any econ course with him!
goat among goats. take his class.
The GOATS of GOATS. Take him he is the best.

Great prof, we had two hour lectures but he made it go through fine and would explain everything well, also was easy to talk to outside of class, final was a bit harder then the midterm but tbf I didn't touch the post midterm chapters problem set till a few days before the exam, just go to class and you'll do well, class avg this term was a B

If you have a chance to take a class with Yan Yan, TAKE THE CLASS. I did awful on the final (mostly my own fault due to bad studying habits) and still ended up with a B. Prof is sweet, genuinely intelligent, caring, and wants you to do well. I genuinely feel motivated to do better thanks to him.
Some call him the GOAT. He is very likeable and lenient

I didn't really know econ had this much math n graph stuff and I've kinda been struggling with it tbh. But Yan explains things in a way that actually makes sense. When I go ask him questions after class he just breaks it down and then it kinda clicks for me. I've honestly learned a lot from that.

Yan Yan is the GOAT. Had him for 103 and 105. I've never written a review before, but he is just one of those guys. He is approachable, nice and clear on grading. You basically just have to show up to class and listen, and you will do well. The midterms and finals are very similar to the lectures. I never bought the textbook, and had no issues.
I have him for 103 and 105 and he is good prof

Yan is a very smart and good professor. He gives explanations and examples that make concepts easy to understand, is kind and caring, and wants everyone to do well. Concepts I'd normally be confused to death by make sense thanks to him. He does give pop quizzes but they're more so about attendance, all you have to do is be there to get at least 50%
I absolutely recommend him.

Yan Yan is the GOAT! He's the professor I've ever had, he's funny, his lectures are engaging, and he CURVES GREAT! He'll explain/clarify concepts for you, change the weightage of exams if you show improvement, and his tests are fair. Do practice problems (especially long answer) and study sets every week, without fail, and you'll be perfectly fine!

An amazing and knowledgeable professor. I understood econ easily through him and he was always available outside of lectures to help. Content wise,103 is easy, just make sure to do textbook questions to understand how to do some long answers questions and understand definitions from the slides for MC. Also he curves great, wonderful professor.

Amazing professor, he's pretty clear when going over concepts and he'll take the time to answer questions clearly in lecture as well. He does give quizzes at random dates but they're extremely easy so free marks. Exams are also fairly easy, mostly technical questions but he gives marks for how well you explain.
Great prof with an awesome vibe. He's super patient, not just in class but especially if you go to office hours. Definitely go if you can.

Fantastic professor who clearly cares about what he's teaching and the students he teaches to. There is a class textbook, but I felt it wasn't mandatory (his lectures are very informative and prepare you well for the practice assignments, which prepare you well for the exams) and is moreso additional content. Will definitely remember this class.

Amazing prof! I had a few friends who didn't take econ in high school found it fairly easy as Yan explains things very well. Furthermore, he is very helpful and caring to when it comes to content you don't understand in tutorials.

Best econ professor, dont be afraid of the econ 103 or 105 title. Super funny, Great lecturer, and super open to helping out and explaining concepts. Often helps out students by weighing different tests lower if you show improvement. Some pop quizzes but if you show up all the time its just free grades. Accents sometimes tough to understand.
Yan Yan is the man, all his tests are fair and everything covered during lectures will be covered in the exams. He is a fair man and an amazing man, Yan Yan the goat.

Super nice guy and explains things really well. Course material was taken straight from the textbook so if you miss a class or don't pay attention just read the textbook/lecture slides and you will do fine. The exams are fair and the material is exactly what he taught in class. Best professor I've had at sfu so far and I definitely recommend him!!

nice guy, very friendly and easy to approach if u don't understand material. all material is posted online, he keeps it simple and straightforward, although he does give surprise participation quizzes sometimes. midterms are semi-difficult but if you actually study what he tells you to and do his practice problems you'll be fine.
He's an awesome lecturer! He throws in jokes during lectures to explain concepts, and honestly, he nails it every time.
Yan Yan is super nice and caring. His explanations are helpful if you listen. Tests are super fair, but the class is graded only on 2 midterms, 1 final and a couple of quizzes.
I struggled with math in the middle parts of this course, but now I'm actually thinking about switching from business to econ. Yan made econ so much fun and helped me see it in a whole new way!

I'll admit, I'm trash at anything math related. My grade is due to me not taking the time to understand the concepts. I will say, it's kinda hard to hear what he says. Bit of a thick accent, I thought he was French at first, and his classes were a bit unorganized. Still, he's a generous marker and I agree that he's one of the better Econ profs.

Yan is a very goofy guy, but in a good way. His lectures are kinda boring and he might go on very long tangents but he explains the topics in great detail and basically will cover almost everything. I would definitely recommend Yan, definitely one of the greatest profs I've had.
Definitely a good professor! Highly recommended!
Tests and lectures contents are basically the same, there are no surprise questions. Choose with confidence!
Very good new teacher!

I feel like shes a hit or miss. People either really like her or hate her. I personally couldnt learn anything from her, I could grasp the concepts she was trying to explain. I failed in her class. Retook it with a different prof and got a 85% so Im blaming the teacher lol.

It's true that Iryna and ECON103 is not easy. But I would recommend Iryna. She gives lecture notes in lecture only. But, you'll find that her notes are very good to study off of. She goes over relevant and specific questions in lecture. So don't miss a lecture. Also, she gives previous midterms which are great to study off of.

Iryna wants to overload information into you, as fast as possible. I asked her two questions the whole semester during lectures and she acted like I was wasting her time, so I never bothered to ask again. She was just regurgitating textbook (badly written one) material onto the projector. The dreadful lectures made me feel super tired and bored.

Iryna was pretty clear to understand despite her Russian accent. She made evening lectures a bit more enjoyable with her high energy and light jokes. Her lectures were understandable but the content is extremely difficult to grasp so studying concepts in your own time is a must! The bad part was that your mark relies on 2 midterms and 1 final exam.

Iryna was pretty clear to understand despite her Russian accent. She made evening lectures a bit more enjoyable with her high energy and light jokes. Her lectures were understandable but the content is extremely difficult to grasp so studying concepts in your own time is a must! The bad part was that your mark relies on 2 midterms and 1 final exam!
